title: Wizards of the Coast
content:
{{Top|rwc}}
{{RealCompany
|image=[[File:Wizards_of_the_Coast_logo.svg]]
|name=Wizards of the Coast
|founded=[[1990]]<ref name="WotC About">{{WizardsCite|url=Company/About.aspx|text=Wizards of the Coast - About Us|archivedate=20130522145904}}</ref>
|founder=[[Wikipedia:Peter Adkison|Peter Adkison]]<ref name="WotC About" />

'''Wizards of the Coast''' (or '''WotC''') was the license-holder and publisher of the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', the ''[[Star Wars Trading Card Game]]'', and ''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]'' until [[May]] [[2010]]. The first Star Wars Roleplaying Game product was released in [[2000]] and the Revised Core Rulebook, containing a new version of the rules was released in [[2002]]. The Star Wars Miniatures game started in [[2004]]. A new edition of the Star Wars Roleplaying Game rules, [[Saga Edition]], was released in [[June]] of [[2007]].
Wizards of the Coast is based in [[Wikipedia:Renton, Washington|Renton]], [[Wikipedia:Washington (U.S. state)|Washington]].

Web content included the [[Message to Spacers]] Series, the Strategy Showcase Series, the [[Dawn of Defiance]] adventures, [[Jedi Counseling]], and a number of other web supplements.

On [[January 28]], 2010 it was announced by Greg Yahn, the Director of Marketing for WOTC, on Wizards of the Coast's official forum that Wizards' would not seek to renew the ''Star Wars'' license with [[Lucasfilm]], and it would lapse in May of that year. Citing "economic downturn", the license to produce all of the company's ''Star Wars'' brand lines would be discontinued.<ref name="Expire">{{WizardsCite|url=default.asp?x=starwars|text=Star Wars: RPG}}</ref><ref name="WotCNews">{{SWArchive|url=games/othergames/news20100128wotc/index.html|text=An Announcement about Star Wars RPGs and Miniature Gaming}}</ref>
